entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's career advising company; should focus on identifying candidate's strongest anchor

entities.name: L-shaped moves
entities.context: Strategy for career transitions: change role then domain (or vice versa) rather than both simultaneously

entities.name: Degrees of freedom
entities.context: Framework for modeling career strategy; changes in domain and function consume degrees of freedom

entities.name: Pattern matching
entities.context: Heuristic used by markets and investors for risk mitigation when evaluating candidates

entities.name: Market acceptability
entities.context: Determined by geometric distance between candidate's current state and target role

entities.name: Fundability
entities.context: Investment viability in career pivots; linked to geometric distance from target

entities.name: Geometric distance
entities.context: Metaphor for measuring difficulty of career transitions across dimensions

entities.name: Diagonal leaps
entities.context: Anti-pattern: simultaneous pivot in both domain and function that breaks anchors

relationships.context: Advising approach to minimize market friction during career transitions
Acceptability determined by distance between current and target state
Fundability determined by same geometric distance metric
Markets use pattern matching to assess candidates, affecting acceptability
L-shaped moves preserve one anchor by using one degree of freedom at a time
Diagonal leaps are the anti-pattern that L-shaped moves solve
Understanding that markets use pattern matching as risk heuristic
